claim: An agent harness is everything around the model that gives it grounding in reality—tools, context management, guardrails, an agent loop, and verification steps that prevent hallucination and ensure reliability.
actionable: Structure agentic systems with explicit harness components: tool registry, guardrails (max steps/tokens), context compaction, and verification steps rather than relying on the model alone.
source_title: Harnesses in AI: A Deep Dive — Tejas Kumar, IBM

claim: A verify step that inspects tool call history can catch agent lies—when an agent claims success but the actual tool calls reveal failure, the harness can enforce honesty.
actionable: Implement a verify step in your agent harness that checks the trace of actual tool calls before accepting the agent's self-reported success claims.
source_title: Harnesses in AI: A Deep Dive — Tejas Kumar, IBM

claim: A harness can programmatically handle external obstacles (like login pages) without changing the prompt, by injecting credentials deterministically when the browser URL matches a login page.
actionable: Build a login handler into your harness that watches browser state and injects credentials/submits forms programmatically rather than expecting the agent to navigate auth flows.
source_title: Harnesses in AI: A Deep Dive — Tejas Kumar, IBM

claim: Context compaction guardrails can trim message history to keep the most recent messages while preserving the system and user prompts, enabling longer agent runs without token explosion.
actionable: Implement a context compressor in your harness that removes middle-history messages when message count exceeds a threshold, keeping system/user prompts and recent context.
source_title: Harnesses in AI: A Deep Dive — Tejas Kumar, IBM

claim: The quality and reliability improvements from a good harness can allow much older models (like GPT-3.5 Turbo) to solve tasks reliably that would otherwise fail.
actionable: When building agent systems, prioritize harness quality over model selection; a strong harness may be more impactful than upgrading to a newer model.
source_title: Harnesses in AI: A Deep Dive — Tejas Kumar, IBM

claim: 2025 was the year of agents; 2026 is the year of harnesses; 2027 may see agents that dynamically generate their own harnesses before execution (self-aware harness generation).
actionable: Expect the industry to shift focus from agent frameworks to robust harness infrastructure; prepare your systems to support dynamic harness creation.
source_title: Harnesses in AI: A Deep Dive — Tejas Kumar, IBM
